name: search_docs
description: Full-text search across the knowledge base. Returns the documents matching the query, ranked by relevance. Use terms in the language of the indexed content.

tag.description: Optional: only keep results that also carry this tag (folder-derived or frontmatter, case-insensitive).

name: read_doc
description: Read the full raw content of a document (.md OR .html). For a .html you get the complete HTML source (not extracted text).

path.description: Relative path of the .md or .html (e.g. notes/example.md, projects/deck-may.html). Use list_tree or search_docs to discover paths.

name: list_tree
description: List the full tree of the knowledge base (metadata only, no content). Handy to understand the folder organization before navigating.

name: recent_docs
description: Recently modified documents, newest first. Handy to know what the user has worked on lately.

name: create_doc
description:
Create a new document. Two formats, pick by content:
• .md — prose, notes, recaps, specs, markdown reports (rendered with marked in the viewer). Default.
• .html — a SELF-CONTAINED styled document: slide deck, dashboard, richly laid-out page with its own CSS/JS. Rendered AS-IS in a viewer iframe sandbox.
RULE: NEVER wrap a full HTML document (<!DOCTYPE html>…) inside a ```html code block of a .md — it would show as raw source. Create a .html instead.
Refuses if the path already exists (create-only, no overwrite). The path must end in .md or .html and be in a valid folder (notes/, projects/, inbox/, etc.).

path.description: Relative path ending in .md or .html (e.g. inbox/note.md, projects/deck-report.html)

content.description: For a .md: full markdown body with an H1 title. For a .html: a complete self-contained HTML page (<!DOCTYPE html>…</html>), inline CSS/JS included.

ai.description: REQUIRED. Identify yourself by your AI FAMILY in lowercase — e.g. 'claude', 'chatgpt', 'gemini' — NOT the model variant (not 'opus', 'gpt-4o'). Attributes this change.

name: edit_doc
description:
Edit an EXISTING document (.md OR .html). Two mutually exclusive modes:
1. Targeted replacement: pass 'old_string' + 'new_string'. 'old_string' must appear exactly once in the document (otherwise an error) — add surrounding context to make it unique. Token-efficient, preferred on large docs (and .html, often big).
2. Full rewrite: pass 'content' (replaces the whole file).
At least one of the two modes is required. The document must already exist (use create_doc to create a new one). Read the doc with read_doc before editing.

path.description: Relative path of the existing .md or .html (e.g. notes/recap.md, projects/deck-may.html)

old_string.description: Patch mode: the exact text to replace, must be unique in the document.

new_string.description: Patch mode: the replacement text (required if old_string is provided).

content.description: Rewrite mode: the full new markdown content. Ignored if old_string is provided.

commit_message.description: Optional. A short, specific summary of WHAT you changed and WHY (e.g. 'corrige le taux de TVA', 'ajoute la section pricing'), like a human changelog line. Becomes the commit subject. Do NOT write generic filler ('update doc', 'edit') — omit it instead and the server falls back to a clear default.

ai.description: REQUIRED. Identify yourself by your AI FAMILY in lowercase — e.g. 'claude', 'chatgpt', 'gemini' — NOT the model variant (not 'opus', 'gpt-4o'). Attributes this change.

name: move_doc
description: Move OR rename an existing document (.md or .html), AUTOMATICALLY rewriting the incoming [[wikilinks]] that point at it (no broken backlinks). Renaming = a move within the same folder (just change the filename in 'to'). Refuses if the target already exists (no overwrite). Read list_tree/search_docs first for the exact path.

from.description: CURRENT relative path of the .md (e.g. notes/draft.md)

to.description: NEW relative .md path (e.g. projects/draft-final.md). Same folder + new name = a plain rename.

ai.description: REQUIRED. Identify yourself by your AI FAMILY in lowercase — e.g. 'claude', 'chatgpt', 'gemini' — NOT the model variant (not 'opus', 'gpt-4o'). Attributes this change.

name: get_links
description: Outgoing [[wikilinks]] of a document: the docs it points to. Lets you traverse the mind's graph forward from a doc.

name: get_backlinks
description: Backlinks of a document: the docs that point TO it via [[wikilinks]]. Find what references a doc.

name: get_mind_topology
description: Bird's-eye view of the whole mind's graph (a summary, not the full graph): doc/edge counts, density, hubs (most-referenced docs), orphans (no link), and the most frequent tags. Call it on first contact with a mind to get oriented before diving in.

name: list_by_tag
description: List the documents carrying a given tag (folder-derived or frontmatter). Navigate the mind by topic, like a human browsing folders.

name: delete_doc
description: Delete a document — SOFT delete: it is moved to a .trash/ folder (reversible), never erased. To rename, use move_doc instead. Refuses on remote mirrors (remotes/). Called by an AI, so the move-to-trash keeps a wrong call recoverable.

path.description: Relative path of the .md/.html to delete

ai.description: REQUIRED. Identify yourself by your AI FAMILY in lowercase — e.g. 'claude', 'chatgpt', 'gemini' — NOT the model variant (not 'opus', 'gpt-4o'). Attributes this change.

name: doc_history
description: Git revision history of a document, newest-first, following renames/moves. The entry point of the time axis: each revision's 'sha' is the token doc_at / doc_diff / doc_revert consume. Also returns the doc's 'created' and 'last_modified' commits. Unlike recent_docs (filesystem mtime, unreliable in cloud where the repo is cloned at boot), this is the truthful per-doc history. since/until/grep are git-native FILTERS, NOT revisions.

since.description: Optional git date filter, e.g. '2 weeks ago' or '2026-06-01'. A date/text filter, NOT a revision.

until.description: Optional git date filter (upper bound), same format as since.

grep.description: Optional: only revisions whose commit subject matches this text.

name: doc_at
description: Read the FULL content of a document as it was at a past point in time — the time-parameterized twin of read_doc (which only reads the current version). Pass EITHER 'rev' (a SHA from doc_history, or HEAD~N) OR 'at' (a date like '2026-05-01' — resolved to the last revision on or before it). Use it to read prior context a diff alone omits.

rev.description: A commit SHA (from doc_history) or HEAD~N. Mutually exclusive with 'at'.

at.description: A date ('2026-05-01', 'last tuesday'): resolves to the doc's last revision on or before it. Use this instead of guessing a SHA.

name: doc_diff
description: What CHANGED in a document. Pass just 'rev' to see what THAT commit changed in the doc (base defaults to the doc's previous version — a 1-arg call). Pass 'base' too to diff between two arbitrary revisions. Returns a unified diff plus added/removed line counts. Handles renames; large diffs are truncated.

rev.description: The newer revision: a SHA or HEAD~N. Default HEAD.

base.description: Optional older revision (SHA or HEAD~N). Omit to diff against the doc's previous version (this commit's change).

name: search_history
description: Search ACROSS TIME, not just the current corpus. Finds the commits where a string entered or left the history (git pickaxe) — answers 'when did we decide X', 'which version introduced this fact', 'find text that was since deleted'. search_docs only sees the present; this sees every past state. Returns matching commits (newest-first) with the doc files they changed.

query.description: The text whose appearance/disappearance you are tracking.

regex.description: True: treat query as a regex matching changed lines (-G). False: exact-string occurrence-count change (-S).

path_prefix.description: Optional: restrict to a folder or file (e.g. 'projets' or 'projets/cdc.md'). Empty = whole mind.

name: changelog
description: Commit-level activity across the whole mind over a recent window: what documents were added/modified/deleted/renamed and when. The corpus-wide complement to per-doc doc_history, and the truthful 'what changed lately' (recent_docs' mtime ranking is flattened to boot-time in cloud). Optionally scope to a folder or filter by author (handy to skip automated sync commits).

author.description: Optional: only commits by this author (substring match), e.g. to exclude bot/sync commits.

path.description: Optional: restrict to a folder or file prefix. Empty = whole mind.

name: doc_blame
description: Per-line provenance of a document: for each line, which commit/author/date last changed it. Answers 'which commit is responsible for THIS exact fact/line'. Pass 'pattern' to blame only the lines containing a string (no line numbers needed), or start/end for a line range. Whole-file blame is capped.

path.description: Relative path of the .md/.html (blamed at the current version).

pattern.description: Optional: only return blamed lines whose text contains this (case-insensitive). The AI-friendly way to target a line without knowing its number.

start.description: Optional 1-based start line of a range.

end.description: Optional end line of the range (omit for start..EOF).

name: doc_revert
description: Restore a document to a past revision: writes that revision's content back as the CURRENT version (a forward-moving change — the in-between history is kept, fully reversible by reverting again). Use after inspecting with doc_history/doc_diff/doc_at. Refuses on remote mirrors (remotes/).

path.description: Relative path of the .md/.html to restore.

rev.description: The revision to restore to: a commit SHA (from doc_history) or HEAD~N.

ai.description: REQUIRED. Identify yourself by your AI FAMILY in lowercase — e.g. 'claude', 'chatgpt', 'gemini' — NOT the model variant (not 'opus', 'gpt-4o'). Attributes this change.
